    status("starting rewrite agent")
    result: ResultMessage | None = None
    try:
        async for message in query(
            prompt=f"Process the document '{title}' into {n_levels} zoom levels. "
                   f"Start by calling get_source.",
            options=options,
        ):
            if isinstance(message, AssistantMessage):
                status(f"agent is writing (attempt {state['attempts'] + 1} of the rewrite)")
            elif isinstance(message, ResultMessage):
                result = message
    except Exception as e:
        hint = ("" if os.environ.get("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY")
                else " — ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is not set, which this deployment requires")
        tail = f" | stderr: {' / '.join(stderr_tail[-3:])}" if stderr_tail else ""
        raise RuntimeError(f"rewrite agent failed: {e}{hint}{tail}") from e

Assistant2026-07-23T22:22:39.226Z

While production builds, the one thing I need from you: an ANTHROPIC_API_KEY for the deployed service — uploads on production will fail with a clear "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is not set" error until it's there. Two easy options:

  1. Add it yourself in the Railway dashboard → fastreader service → Variables → ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, or
  2. Paste/export it here (e.g. ! export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-… or just tell me it's in your shell env) and I'll set it on the service and redeploy.

Phase 2 status: everything else is done and merged to main —

  • Pipeline agent (scripts/pipeline.py): Agent SDK on claude-sonnet-5 with two in-process tools — get_source (numbered paragraphs + computed level plan) and submit_rewrites (runs the assembler + validator; errors bounce back into the agent loop until the document is clean). Proven live: it processed The Yellow Wallpaper into 4 validated levels with genuinely great names — In one breath (106w) → The creeping shape (641w) → Behind the pattern (2,282w) → Every word (6,085w) — and it reads beautifully in the reader.
  • Upload API + job store: POST /api/upload, per-job JSON status files, background processing, 24 backend tests green.
  • Upload UI: "Add a document" card → modal (paste or .txt/.md file) → live processing card polling every 2s → the finished book appears in the library.
  • Container fixes found by smoke-testing: the SDK ships a self-contained CLI binary (dropped a planned ~300MB Node layer), and Claude Code refuses bypassPermissions as root — fixed with the sandbox flag scoped to the pipeline subprocess.
  • Railway: persistent volume fastreader-data mounted at /data; deploy from the merged main is building now — my poller will confirm when the jobs API is live.

Also: origin/main had no new commits from your UI worktrees yet — I merged from it before integrating, and I'll pull again before any future push.

+
Phase 2 (uploads + Agent SDK rewrite pipeline on `claude-sonnet-5`) is deployed as of 2026-07-23:
- Volume `fastreader-data` (id `8bbaa46b-c24c-4aed-95a2-02821a76cd23`) mounted at `/data` (`DATA_DIR`).
- The pipeline (`scripts/pipeline.py`) spawns the SDK's bundled CLI with `IS_SANDBOX=1` (container runs as root; Claude Code refuses bypassPermissions as root otherwise).
- **`ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` must be set on the service for uploads to work** — as of deploy time the user had not yet provided it; uploads fail with a clear error until then.
Tool reply2026-07-23T22:23:08.756Z
The file /Users/honglei/.claude/projects/-Users-honglei-code-fastreader/memory/fastreader-railway-deployment.md has been updated successfully.
Assistant2026-07-23T22:23:19.918Z

Phase 2 is live on production: https://fastreader-production.up.railway.app/ — the jobs API is up, the volume is mounted, and the "Add a document" flow is on the live site.

One item remains, and it's the one only you can provide: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY on the Railway service. Until it's set, uploads on production fail fast with a clear "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is not set" message on the job card (verified in the container smoke test). Add it in the Railway dashboard under the fastreader service → Variables, or hand it to me and I'll set it and run a live upload test to close the loop. Everything else — pipeline agent, upload API, UI, tests, volume — is done, merged to main, and verified.
